summary: The diff implements most of the required features: backend favorites methods (REQ-5/6/7) via backend/main.mo with OrderedMap, the NOC mini-widget component and hook (REQ-9) with polling and CSS animations, and the KDTrustPage integration. However, the useKDTrustFavorites hook update (REQ-8) is not directly visible in the diff, and the backend stable variable implementation uses OrderedMap rather than the specified stable HashMap pattern, which may affect upgrade-safety guarantees.

missing_requirements.requirement: Update frontend useKDTrustFavorites hook to call backend getFavorites/setFavorites for authenticated users, merge localStorage on login, and clear localStorage after merge.
missing_requirements.reason: The file frontend/src/hooks/useKDTrustFavorites.ts is not listed as an added or modified file in the diff. The frontend-file-summaries.txt mentions useGetFavorites/useSetFavorites hooks in useQueries.ts but does not confirm the useKDTrustFavorites hook itself was updated to implement the login merge logic and localStorage clearing behavior.

missing_requirements.requirement: Use a stable HashMap (stable var) keyed by Principal for upgrade-safe favorites storage.
missing_requirements.reason: The diff shows backend/main.mo uses OrderedMap (not a stable var HashMap). The migration.mo file suggests a migration pattern is used, but the main.mo diff is truncated and does not clearly show a stable var favoritesStore declaration, raising concern about whether the AC for stable variables is fully met.

hallucinated_features.description: A backend/migration.mo module was added to handle actor state migration between upgrades.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not mention a separate migration module. It only requires stable variables in the main actor. Adding a dedicated migration.mo is beyond the specification.

hallucinated_features.description: NOC status polling only activates for authenticated users due to an isAuthenticated check in useNOCLiveStatus.
hallucinated_features.reason: REQ-9 does not restrict the NOC widget to authenticated users only. The hook returns FALLBACK_STATUS when the user is not authenticated, but the requirement does not specify this restriction.
